stories.headline: Cursor March 2026: cloud-isolated parallel subagents, BugBot graduates with 70%+ resolution rate
stories.summary: Cursor moved agents into isolated cloud VMs, enabling 10–20 subagents to build features, run tests, and open merge-ready PRs simultaneously. BugBot is now GA with 70%+ resolution rate, and over 30% of Cursor's own merged PRs are now created by autonomous agents.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to parallel Roman aqueduct construction
stories.lateral_explanation: Roman engineers ran multiple aqueduct channels in parallel, each isolated, so failure of one wouldn't contaminate the rest — the same redundancy logic that makes Cursor's cloud VM isolation powerful. Each agent's context is hermetic, making concurrency safe rather than chaotic. The Romans solved the

stories.headline: LangChain ships Open-SWE: async open-source software engineering agent for complex coding tasks
stories.summary: LangChain released Open-SWE, a fully asynchronous coding agent that parallelises file edits, test runs, and context updates without stalling the main workflow. It is designed for large codebases with complex dependencies where blocking operations create critical-path delays.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to UNIX fork-exec model
stories.lateral_explanation: The architectural philosophy behind Open-SWE is the same one Ken Thompson and Dennis Ritchie baked into UNIX in 1969: spawn child processes, let them run independently, collect results — don't block the parent. The async agent is essentially UNIX's fork() call ported from operating systems into LLM-

stories.headline: LangChain + NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint ships: enterprise deep research on Deep Agents + speculative execution
stories.summary: LangChain and NVIDIA released the AI-Q Blueprint, a production enterprise research system combining LangGraph's durable execution with NVIDIA's parallel and speculative execution for complex multi-hour research workflows. The system ranked top on deep research benchmarks by decomposing tasks into concurrent sub-queries with human-in-the-loop checkpoints.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Renaissance workshop delegation
stories.lateral_explanation: Renaissance masters like Raphael ran large workshops where apprentices handled background figures, drapery, and architectural details in parallel while the master focused on faces and compositions — the same division AI-Q Blueprint formalises. NVIDIA handles the speculative parallel execution, LangG

stories.headline: Google releases Gemini 3.1 Flash Live in developer preview: real-time multimodal voice + video for agents
stories.summary: Google opened Gemini 3.1 Flash Live to developers through Google AI Studio, targeting low-latency audio, video, and tool use for AI agents that need real-time conversational interaction. The model supports function calling during live voice sessions with responses under 200ms.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to UN simultaneous interpretation
stories.lateral_explanation: UN simultaneous interpreters must process speech, hold partial sentences in short-term memory, translate in real-time, and speak without pausing the source — a cognitive feat requiring a completely different neural circuit than reading and translating. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is the first model archit

stories.headline: Anthropic confirms Claude Mythos: new model class beyond Opus 4.6 with dramatically higher coding, reasoning, and cybersecurity scores
stories.summary: Anthropic confirmed that Claude Mythos — previously leaked — is real and being tested with select enterprise customers. Mythos represents a new tier above the Opus line, achieving significantly higher scores across software coding, academic reasoning, and cybersecurity evaluations, described as a step change rather than incremental improvement.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the Cambrian explosion
stories.lateral_explanation: The Cambrian explosion wasn't a smooth gradient of improvement — it was a phase transition where body plans that had been impossible suddenly became viable simultaneously. Claude Mythos appears to be a similar discontinuity: not better at the same tasks, but entering categories like autonomous cyber

stories.headline: GPT-5.4 mini Thinking mode rolls out to ChatGPT Free and Go users via the + menu
stories.summary: OpenAI began rolling out GPT-5.4 mini to all ChatGPT Free and Go users, giving 200M+ monthly active users access to chain-of-thought reasoning via the Thinking toggle in the + menu. The rollout democratises step-by-step reasoning for users who previously had access only to direct-answer models.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the paperback revolution
stories.lateral_explanation: When Penguin Books launched paperbacks in 1935 at sixpence each, critics worried it would cheapen literature; instead it created a generation of readers who graduated to hardcovers. OpenAI's free Thinking mode rollout follows the same logic: chain-of-thought reasoning as an entry point, not a produc

stories.headline: Datadog Bits AI SRE upgrade: deeper reasoning engine, incidents resolved 90% faster in production
stories.summary: Datadog updated its always-on SRE agent with a deeper reasoning engine that iteratively forms hypotheses, gathers telemetry, and cross-references observability data to pinpoint root causes in minutes. In production, Bits AI SRE reduces mean-time-to-resolution by 90% without requiring on-call engineers to manually correlate dashboards and logs.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Sherlock Holmes's abductive method
stories.lateral_explanation: Holmes didn't wait for complete evidence before forming hypotheses — he actively generated and tested conjectures against available data, iterating rapidly toward a conclusion. Bits AI SRE's iterative hypothesis formation is abductive reasoning industrialised: each telemetry query either confirms or

stories.headline: GitHub Agent HQ launches: multiple AI coding agents in parallel with full plan, progress, and file visibility
stories.summary: GitHub introduced Agent HQ, letting developers run multiple AI coding agents side-by-side on different codebase areas, with real-time visibility into each agent's plan, progress state, and file modifications. The interface enables developers to monitor agent work like a sprint board — reviewing, redirecting, or approving branches before merge.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the Venetian Arsenal shipyard
stories.lateral_explanation: The Venetian Arsenal at its peak ran dozens of specialised workshops simultaneously — keel laying, rigging, cannon mounting — each visible to the Arsenal master from a central walkway. The Arsenal master's walkway is GitHub Agent HQ: one view across simultaneous specialised work, with the authority

stories.headline: JetBrains Central: first IDE-native control layer for orchestrating multi-agent workflows across a codebase
stories.summary: JetBrains launched JetBrains Central, a coordination layer embedded in its IDE ecosystem that manages context routing, tool permissions, and output validation across multiple AI coding agents in the same project. Rather than independent contexts, JetBrains Central maintains a shared project model all agents read and write to, preventing context drift and conflicting edits.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to air traffic control
stories.lateral_explanation: Air traffic controllers don't fly planes — they maintain a shared model of airspace that all pilots reference simultaneously, preventing conflicts through centralised context rather than direct coordination between aircraft. JetBrains Central is the ATC tower for AI coding agents: the shared airspac

stories.headline: xAI Grok Build: 8 simultaneous coding agents with built-in conflict resolution for overlapping file edits
stories.summary: xAI released Grok Build, running up to 8 agents in parallel on different codebase parts, with automatic conflict detection and resolution when agents attempt simultaneous edits to the same file region. The system uses priority queues and change-set merging — similar to distributed version control — handling most concurrency conflicts without developer intervention.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval manuscript scriptoria
stories.lateral_explanation: Monastic scriptoria solved the same problem centuries before git: multiple scribes copying different sections of the same illuminated manuscript, with a master corrector reconciling inconsistencies in chapter headings, cross-references, and decorative schemes. Grok Build's conflict resolution is the

stories.headline: Accenture + Databricks Business Group: 25,000 certified pros and Albertsons merchant twin pricing agent live
stories.summary: Accenture and Databricks launched a dedicated business group backed by 25,000 Databricks-trained professionals, with Albertsons already running a live merchant twin — an AI agent performing pricing lookback analysis, category planning, and promotion calendar optimisation autonomously. The agent puts hyper-precision pricing in the hands of category managers, driving margin expansion without additional headcount.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to double-entry bookkeeping revolution
stories.lateral_explanation: When Luca Pacioli formalised double-entry bookkeeping in 1494, it didn't replace merchants — it gave every merchant the analytical power previously accessible only to the largest trading houses. The Albertsons merchant twin does the same for category pricing: what previously required a team of data

stories.headline: PwC rolls out Learning Collective nationally: 30 paired AI+human skills, grads in residential AI immersion
stories.summary: PwC launched its Learning Collective across the US with 30 essential skills defined as inseparable AI-human pairs — each AI capability taught alongside a corresponding human skill. All new US graduates now enter a week-long residential AI immersion, while all partners and managing directors complete an 8-week executive AI coaching track.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval apprenticeship guild system
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval guilds didn't train apprentices in craft alone — they embedded craftsmanship within a social and ethical code, believing technical skill divorced from judgment produced dangerous practitioners. PwC's paired AI+human curriculum is the same wager: that AI capability without judgment is a liab

stories.headline: PLDT deploys ERICA: AI risk management agent across Philippines' largest telecom's full operations
stories.summary: PLDT deployed ERICA, an AI agent managing risk analysis, compliance checking, and operational anomaly detection across the company's full infrastructure and business operations. ERICA runs continuous risk assessments replacing a manually-intensive process that previously required large analyst teams aggregating weekly reports.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Great Barrier Reef's immune response
stories.lateral_explanation: Coral polyps detect chemical and biological threats through distributed sensor networks across thousands of square kilometers, triggering localised immune responses before damage spreads — without a central nervous system making decisions. ERICA implements the same distributed vigilance principle ac

stories.headline: Oracle Fusion Agentic Applications live: 22 autonomous agents run supply chain, procurement, and finance
stories.summary: Oracle shipped 22 production-ready AI agents integrated into its Fusion suite, automating supply chain management, procurement workflows, financial close, and vendor management autonomously within existing ERP systems. Enterprise customers report supply chain exception handling shifting from 3-day manual review cycles to sub-hour autonomous resolution.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Ford River Rouge vertical integration
stories.lateral_explanation: Ford's River Rouge plant took raw iron ore in at one end and shipped finished cars out the other — no handoffs, no external dependencies, no idle inventory. Oracle's 22 agents create the same vertically integrated flow inside the enterprise: materials, money, and contracts move through automated pro

stories.headline: Datadog Bits AI Dev Agent GA: monitors CI logs, autonomously generates PRs, iterates until tests pass
stories.summary: Datadog made Bits AI Dev Agent generally available — an autonomous coding agent that watches CI/CD logs for failures, identifies root causes, generates pull requests with fixes, and iterates until all tests pass. In production, the agent reduces average time-to-fix for CI failures from 2.3 hours to 14 minutes.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Deep Space 1's self-healing software
stories.lateral_explanation: Deep Space 1, launched in 1998, ran AI software that autonomously diagnosed hardware faults and rewrote its own execution plan without ground control — because light-speed communication delays made human intervention impossible. Datadog's Dev Agent applies the same autonomy principle to CI pipelines

stories.headline: Cursor discloses AI agents write 30%+ of own merged pull requests — first software company to report this milestone
stories.summary: Cursor publicly disclosed that more than 30% of its own merged production pull requests are now created by the autonomous agents running inside its platform, making it the first software company to formally report a self-generated code share approaching majority scale. The disclosure signals agentic coding has passed from developer productivity tool to active software contributor.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the printing press becoming a publisher
stories.lateral_explanation: Gutenberg's press was first conceived as a tool for human scribes to produce faster — but within decades the press itself began originating documents, pamphlets, and newspapers that no individual scribe had authored. Cursor's 30% milestone is the equivalent moment: the tool has graduated from assist

stories.headline: MCP protocol reaches 97 million installs: Morgan Stanley, Klarna, BBVA confirmed enterprise deployments
stories.summary: The Model Context Protocol hit 97 million installs in March 2026, with Morgan Stanley, Klarna, and BBVA confirmed as production deployments using MCP to connect AI agents to enterprise data systems, CRM platforms, and internal APIs. MCP moved from architectural specification to de facto enterprise standard in under 18 months.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to TCP/IP standardisation of 1983
stories.lateral_explanation: Before TCP/IP was standardised, every network ran proprietary protocols that couldn't talk to each other — ARPANET, BITNET, and dozens of corporate intranets were isolated islands. MCP's 97M install milestone marks the same convergence point: the moment the AI agent ecosystem stops being fragmented

stories.headline: Amazon Bedrock Stateful Runtime: persistent memory and tool state for long-running enterprise agent workflows
stories.summary: AWS announced the Stateful Runtime Environment for Amazon Bedrock, enabling AI agents to maintain persistent memory, tool state, and task context across workflows spanning hours or days — solving the context reset problem that forced enterprises to design all agent tasks within single session windows. It targets complex processes like regulatory audits, procurement approvals, and multi-vendor contract workflows.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to working memory in cognitive science
stories.lateral_explanation: Cognitive scientists classify working memory as the bottleneck of human intelligence — tasks that exceed working memory capacity require external scaffolding like notes, diagrams, and checklists. Amazon's Stateful Runtime is that external scaffolding institutionalised: persistent agent memory that r

stories.headline: 72% of Global 2000 companies now operating AI agents beyond experimental phase — March 2026 benchmark
stories.summary: March 2026 data shows 72% of Global 2000 companies have moved AI agents into operational deployments beyond testing, with multi-agent orchestration emerging as the dominant architectural pattern. For the first time, more large global enterprises are running production AI agents than are still in pilot mode.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the Copernican tipping point
stories.lateral_explanation: Before Copernicus, geocentrism was not just a belief — it was the organisational framework of calendars, navigation, and theology. The heliocentric model didn't win through debate; it won when more institutions needed it to work than needed it to be false. The 72% threshold is similar: AI agents hav

stories.headline: LangChain production data: multi-agent Deep Agents outperform solo agents by 340% on complex enterprise tasks
stories.summary: LangChain CEO Harrison Chase shared production data showing that multi-agent Deep Agents configurations with specialised sub-agents for planning, execution, and validation outperform single-agent approaches by 340% on tasks requiring more than 20 reasoning steps. The data comes from production deployments across LangChain's enterprise customer base.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to ant colony collective intelligence
stories.lateral_explanation: Individual fire ants are poor problem solvers — they make random, inefficient decisions. But colonies exhibit sophisticated navigation, construction, and resource allocation that emerges from simple interaction rules between specialised castes. LangChain's 340% improvement follows the same principle

stories.headline: Domingo de Ramos en Las Letras: la Procesión de los Estudiantes recorre el barrio de Cervantes, Lope y Quevedo
stories.summary: La Procesión de los Estudiantes — portando el Cristo del siglo XVIII de Luis Salvador Carmona — recorre hoy el Barrio de las Letras, donde Cervantes murió en la calle Cervantes y Lope de Vega escribió en su casa-museo. Es una de las pocas zonas de Madrid donde la memoria literaria del Siglo de Oro permanece inscrita en la topografía misma.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to pilgrimage as literary device
stories.lateral_explanation: Every major literary tradition has pilgrimage as structural metaphor — from the Canterbury Tales to the Quijote, movement through known space produces unexpected encounters and transformations. The Procesión de los Estudiantes through the streets where those same literary figures actually lived coll

stories.headline: Cabinas Literarias en Usera: poesía participativa a través de cabinas telefónicas reconvertidas
stories.summary: Una instalación en Usera transforma antiguas cabinas telefónicas en puntos de escucha poética, invitando a transeúntes a descolgarse el auricular y escuchar textos literarios. La pieza usa el anacronismo del dispositivo para hacer de la pausa urbana un acto de lectura involuntaria.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to oral tradition transmission
stories.lateral_explanation: Before literacy, every culture transmitted knowledge through structured oral repetition in specific spatial contexts — the agora, the fireplace, the market square. Usera's Cabinas Literarias short-circuits the visual bias of modern reading and returns poetry to its original medium: the heard word in

stories.headline: Festival de Bach de Madrid abre hoy: interpretación histórica en 12 espacios hasta el 20 de abril
stories.summary: El Festival de Bach de Madrid arranca hoy con tres semanas de música sacra barroca en interpretación histórica — instrumentos de época, afinación de 415 Hz, Pasiones completas en iglesias del centro — más conferencias sobre arquitectura acústica y composición en el Barroco tardío.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to mathematical structure of polyphony
stories.lateral_explanation: Bach's counterpoint follows rules so precise they can be expressed as formal grammar — the same rules Leibniz was developing in mathematics simultaneously, in the same century. The festival's historical interpretation is not nostalgia; it is an experiment in reconstructing a cognitive environment: t

stories.headline: Yllana estrena sátira del siglo XVIII en el Gran Teatro CaixaBank: teatro físico como crítica política
stories.summary: Yllana presenta una sátira musical ambientada en el siglo XVIII que usa el disfraz histórico para comentar el presente. La compañía lleva tres décadas cultivando la tradición del esperpento madrileño: la exageración grotesca como instrumento de verdad.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to commedia dell'arte's subversive masks
stories.lateral_explanation: Commedia dell'arte characters like Pantalone and the Dottore were socially specific caricatures — audiences saw their landlords, their doctors, their judges, grotesquely magnified. Yllana's 18th century setting uses the same mask logic: historical distance makes contemporary critique more visible, n

stories.headline: Plaza Mayor: 'Cinco Siglos de Devoción Madrileña' — las cofradías como productores culturales del Madrid pre-industrial
stories.summary: Una exposición en Plaza Mayor rastrea cómo las cofradías madrileñas en cinco siglos financiaron esculturas, encargaron música, y construyeron el patrimonio visual de la ciudad como productores culturales activos. La muestra sitúa la Semana Santa como industria artística que generó los mayores encargos del Madrid pre-industrial.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the Medici as cultural commissioners
stories.lateral_explanation: The Medicis' genius was understanding that religious patronage and cultural production were the same act: commissioning art was simultaneously an act of faith, civic display, and economic investment. Madrid's cofradías operated the same triple logic — their processions were devotion, spectacle, and

stories.headline: Canvas IgniteAI Agent free globally through September: autonomous rubric generation and grading for educators
stories.summary: Instructure released IgniteAI, an AI agent in Canvas that autonomously generates grading rubrics, aligns course content to learning outcomes, and handles structured grading tasks — with humans kept in the loop for final grade decisions. The agent is free for US and Latin American educators through June 2026 and globally through September.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Prussian grading system's administrative origins
stories.lateral_explanation: The Prussian system that most Western schools still use was designed not for pedagogy but for industrial workforce sorting — it created the administrative burden IgniteAI now automates because the system was never designed around teaching efficiency. The irony is that AI is removing the administrati

stories.headline: Anthropic + Teach For All: 100,000 educators in 63 countries build classroom AI tools with Claude Pro access
stories.summary: Anthropic partnered with Teach For All to give 100,000 teachers and alumni across its 63-country network Claude Pro access and structured AI literacy training, including a climate curriculum built in Liberia and a math game developed in Bangladesh. The Claude Lab component gives teachers monthly access to the Anthropic product team.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Jesuit pedagogy export model
stories.lateral_explanation: The Jesuits in the 16th century didn't build a single great school — they built a replicable method, the Ratio Studiorum, that 800 schools across 4 continents adapted to local context. Anthropic and Teach For All follow the same architecture: a replicable AI literacy framework that 63 country networ

stories.headline: AWS AI & ML Scholars Challenge Phase opens: 100,000 learners on path to AI Practitioner certification
stories.summary: AWS opened the Challenge Phase of its AI & ML Scholars 2026 program (March 24–June 24), targeting 100,000 learners outside traditional tech pathways toward the AWS Certified AI Practitioner credential through AWS Skill Builder. The initiative explicitly targets career changers, community college students, and workers in AI-disrupted sectors.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to the GI Bill's credential democratisation
stories.lateral_explanation: The 1944 GI Bill didn't just fund education — it recalibrated who was considered credentialed: nearly 8 million veterans gained degrees and certifications that reframed the social contract between credentials and social mobility. AWS Scholars is attempting a similar recalibration: industrialising ac

stories.headline: Instructure + OpenAI global partnership: AI experiences natively embedded in Canvas for 40+ million students
stories.summary: Instructure and OpenAI formalised a global partnership to embed OpenAI's models directly into Canvas's learning management infrastructure, enabling AI-powered tutoring, content generation, and assessment feedback within the same interface students already use. The integration targets Canvas's 40+ million user base across higher education and K-12.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to textbook oligopoly's curriculum lock-in
stories.lateral_explanation: A small number of textbook publishers long controlled not just what students read, but how subjects were sequenced and assessed — because the textbook was also the assessment infrastructure. The Canvas-OpenAI integration creates the same bundled infrastructure effect: whoever controls the LMS layer

stories.headline: Inside Higher Ed: agentic AI can now complete entire university courses — academia has no response framework
stories.summary: Inside Higher Ed documented cases where AI agents — given course syllabi, assignment briefs, and database access — completed full semester-length courses with passing grades, raising structural questions about what assessment is measuring in an agentic world. Unlike text generation, agentic completion bypasses detection systems because the agent navigates the LMS and adapts to feedback the way a student would.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to printing press and collapse of oral examination
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval universities assessed students through oral disputation precisely because written reproduction didn't prove knowledge — a student could copy a manuscript without understanding it. The printing press forced universities to develop new assessment forms; AI agents completing courses is forcing

stories.headline: Canvas Career reaches broad availability: skills-first AI learning for workforce, 73% of workers feel unprepared
stories.summary: Instructure expanded Canvas Career — its AI-powered, skills-first platform for adult workforce development — to broad availability, targeting the 73% of workers who feel unprepared to adapt to AI-driven career disruption. Canvas Career maps learning to in-demand skills rather than course credits, with outcome tracking across business, government, and continuing education.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Great Exhibition of 1851's skills anxiety
stories.lateral_explanation: Prince Albert's Great Exhibition was partly driven by British anxiety that artisan skills were becoming obsolete in the face of industrial machinery — it was as much a workforce development exercise as a celebration. The 1851 anxiety was accurate in its diagnosis: the skills gap was real and require

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick's Wharton research on practical human-AI collaboration directly mirrors the enterprise patterns David has been tracking — especially the LangChain finding that human-in-the-loop beats full autonomy. This is the most evidence-backed book on what working alongside AI actually feels like day-to-day.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: With AI agent infrastructure maturing fast and MCP becoming standard protocol, Huyen's systems-level framework for production AI engineering is the most practical guide to the full stack — from evals to deployment patterns to agent orchestration. O'Reilly's most-read AI engineering book in 2026.
